back_translation: 'But now, O Job, hear my words and listen to all my speech.
Behold, I will open my mouth; my tongue is ready to speak.
My words come from an upright heart, and my lips sincerely speak what I know.
The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
Refute me if you can; prepare your case and confront me.
Behold, before God I am just like you; I too was formed from clay.
Surely no fear of me should terrify you, and my hand will not be heavy upon you.
But you have spoken in my hearing, and I have heard the sound of your words:
'I am pure, without transgression; I am clean, and there is no iniquity in me.
Yet God finds occasions against me; He regards me as His enemy.
He puts my feet in the stocks and watches all my paths.'
Behold, in this you are not right; I will answer you, for God is greater than man.
Why do you complain to Him that He does not answer any of man's words?
For God speaks in one way, and then in another, but no one notices.
In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men as they slumber on their beds,
then He opens their ears and seals warnings upon them,
to turn man away from wrongdoing and to restrain him from pride,
to preserve his soul from the Pit and his life from perishing by the sword.
Man is also chastened with pain on his bed, with constant suffering in his bones,
so that he loathes food, and his soul detests his favorite meal.
His flesh wastes away until it cannot be seen, and his once-hidden bones protrude.
His soul draws near to the Pit, and his life to those who bring death.
Yet if there is an angel on his side, a mediator, one in a thousand, to tell a man what is right for him,
to be gracious to him and say, 'Spare him from going down to the Pit; I have found a ransom for him,'
then his flesh becomes fresh like a child's; he returns to the days of his youth.
He prays to God, and He shows him favor; God restores his righteousness to him, and he sees God's face with shouts of joy.
Then he sings before men, saying, 'I have sinned and perverted what was right, but I did not receive the punishment I deserved.
He has redeemed my soul from going down to the Pit, and my life will see the light.'
Behold, God does all these things to a man, two or even three times,
to bring back his soul from the Pit, that he may be given the light of life.
Pay attention, Job, listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
But if you have something to say, answer me; speak up, for I would like to vindicate you.
But if not, then listen to me; be quiet, and I will teach you wisdom.'
